Code context:
```
(no snippet)
```

### Fix 3
Lines: 57, 65
Rule [sd-icon-children-to-prop-button]:
In PatternFly v6, icon content in <Button> must be passed to the `icon` prop instead of as children.

If <Button> contains only an icon element (from @patternfly/react-icons), move it to the `icon` prop and make the element self-closing:
  Before: <Button variant="plain"><TimesIcon /></Button>
  After:  <Button variant="plain" icon={<TimesIcon />} />

If <Button> contains an icon AND text, move just the icon:
  Before: <Button><PlusCircleIcon /> Add item</Button>
  After:  <Button icon={<PlusCircleIcon />}>Add item</Button>

Incident context:
  componentName: Button
  module: @patternfly/react-core
  parentFrom: @patternfly/react-core
  parentName: FormFieldGroupHeader

Fix strategy:
Strategy: LlmAssisted
Component: Button
Prop: icon

This rule affects multiple locations in the file. Apply ALL steps together as one logical change.

Code contexts:
```
  (line 57):
   56              <>
   57                <Button
   58                  data-test="remove-all-ingress"

  (line 65):
   64                </Button>
   65                <Button data-test="add-ingress" onClick={addIngressRule} variant="secondary">
   66                  {t('Add ingress rule')}

```

Instructions:
1. Read the file at /home/jmontleo/semver-analyzer/build/batch-run-20260604T185102/networking-console-plugin/source/src/views/networkpolicies/new/components/NetworkPolicyFormIngress.tsx
2. Process each fix INDEPENDENTLY in sequence. For each fix:
   a. Identify the exact code affected (line number and affected element)
   b. Determine the specific change needed (add/remove/rename identifiers, update references)
   c. Track all changes for the final write
3. Make the minimum edits necessary — do not change unrelated code, but DO clean up any artifacts caused by your changes (e.g., remove imports that are no longer referenced, delete dead declarations)
4. After determining your changes, reason through any functional side effects: verify that your edits preserve the existing behavior of the surrounding code. If a migration change makes existing variables, state, or logic redundant, clean up the affected code so the file remains correct and consistent.
5. Do NOT revert any changes that were already applied in previous passes
6. Write the fixed file once with ALL changes from every fix applied
7. REQUIRED: If companion test files are listed above, read EACH one now. Do NOT skip this step. For each test file, check whether your changes to the main file would cause test failures (e.g., portal rendering means getByText/getByRole won't find dropdown content, renamed attributes break selectors). If a test would break, apply the fix and write the updated test file.


Before writing, reason through each fix step by step to ensure nothing is missed. Then read the file, make the edits, and write it.

After writing the file, produce a '## Changes Applied' section that lists each change you made, each fix that was already applied (no change needed), and each fix you could not apply (with a brief reason). This summary is used by subsequent processing steps.

I'll start by analyzing each fix, then read the file to apply them.

**Fix 1**: `Button.variant="link"` and `variant="secondary"` - The value `"link"` and `"secondary"` are both still valid in the new type union, so no code change is needed. The type just added `"stateful"` as a new option.

**Fix 2**: `FormFieldGroupExpandable` signature changed from `React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>` to `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onToggle'>`. Need to check if `onToggle` is used on any `FormFieldGroupExpandable` instance.

**Fix 3**: Move icon children of `<Button>` to the `icon` prop. Need to examine the actual JSX to see if there are icons as children.

Let me read the file:

Now let me analyze each fix against the actual code:

**Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): Lines 61 and 65 use `variant="link"` and `variant="secondary"`. Both values are still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was adding `'stateful'`. **No code change needed.**

**Fix 2** (FormFieldGroupExpandable signature changed from `React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>` to `Omit<React.HTMLProps<HTMLDivElement>, 'onToggle'>`): Looking at line 51-81, `FormFieldGroupExpandable` is used with props: `className`, `header`, `isExpanded`, `toggleAriaLabel`. None of these are `onToggle`. **No code change needed** — the component doesn't use the removed `onToggle` prop.

**Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop on Button): Looking at lines 57-67, neither Button contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`:
- Lines 57-64: `<Button variant="link">{t('Remove all')}</Button>` — children are text only.
- Line 65: `<Button variant="secondary">{t('Add ingress rule')}</Button>` — children are text only.

**No code change needed** — there are no icon children in any Button.

All three fixes target code that is already compatible with PF v6. No changes are required.

## Changes Applied

1. **Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The values `"link"` and `"secondary"` are both still valid in the new type union. The only change was the addition of `"stateful"` as a new option.

2. **Fix 2** (FormFieldGroupExpandable signature changed — `onToggle` removed from props): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The `FormFieldGroupExpandable` usage on line 51 does not use `onToggle`, so the type narrowing (`Omit<..., 'onToggle'>`) has no impact.

3. **Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **Already compatible — no change needed.** Neither Button (lines 57–64 and line 65) contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Both Buttons have only text children (`{t('Remove all')}` and `{t('Add ingress rule')}`).

**Fix 1** (Button.variant type changed): Lines 61 and 65 use `variant="link"` and `variant="secondary"`. Both values are still valid in the new type union (`'control' | 'danger' | 'link' | 'plain' | 'primary' | 'secondary' | 'stateful' | 'tertiary' | 'warning'`). The only change was adding `'stateful'`. **No code change needed.**

None of these are `onToggle`. **No code change needed** — the component doesn't use the removed `onToggle` prop.

**Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop on Button): Looking at lines 57-67, neither Button contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`:
- Lines 57-64: `<Button variant="link">{t('Remove all')}</Button>` — children are text only.

All three fixes target code that is already compatible with PF v6. No changes are required.

2. **Fix 2** (FormFieldGroupExpandable signature changed — `onToggle` removed from props): **Already compatible — no change needed.** The `FormFieldGroupExpandable` usage on line 51 does not use `onToggle`, so the type narrowing (`Omit<..., 'onToggle'>`) has no impact.

3. **Fix 3** (Icon children to `icon` prop on Button): **Already compatible — no change needed.** Neither Button (lines 57–64 and line 65) contains icon elements from `@patternfly/react-icons`. Both Buttons have only text children (`{t('Remove all')}` and `{t('Add ingress rule')}`).
